channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
item.description: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.
item.content:encoded:
In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.

(Image credit: sorbetto/Digital Vision Vectors)
item.dc:creator: Alex Olgin

item.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
item.description: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
item.content:encoded:
Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.

(Image credit: Hulton Archive)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: How a fertilizer shortage caused by the Iran war could affect U.S. food prices
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted shipments of fertilizer and natural gas, a key component in fertilizer manufacturing. It's unlikely to cause major price hikes for U.S. grocery shoppers.
item.content:encoded:
The closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted shipments of fertilizer and natural gas, a key component in fertilizer manufacturing. It's unlikely to cause major price hikes for U.S. grocery shoppers.

(Image credit: Allison Joyce)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: How young people feel about American identity, on the nation's 250th birthday
item.description: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.content:encoded: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.dc:creator: Janet W. Lee

item.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
item.description: The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.
item.content:encoded:
The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Heat threatens U.S. 250th celebrations, Russian advances slow as Ukraine hits back, infighting risks derailing Democrats' House hopes.
item.content:encoded: Heat threatens U.S. 250th celebrations, Russian advances slow as Ukraine hits back, infighting risks derailing Democrats' House hopes.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Some states rolling back worker heat protections
item.description: The heat dome encasing much of the eastern U.S. is reviving concerns about protecting workers from the heat. Many states have laws in place but some, including Florida, have rolled them back.
item.content:encoded: The heat dome encasing much of the eastern U.S. is reviving concerns about protecting workers from the heat. Many states have laws in place but some, including Florida, have rolled them back.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Meszaros

item.title: Designer of 50-star flag looks back at his high school project
item.description: The designer of today's American flag remembers the day he created it for a student project.
item.content:encoded: The designer of today's American flag remembers the day he created it for a student project.
item.dc:creator: Kerrie Hillman

item.title: Croatia vs. Portugal: What happened in those final, chaotic minutes
item.description: After 90 minutes of play, the electronic board signaled 10 minutes of added time, and it was difficult to imagine at that point just how much drama would be packed into the coming minutes.
item.content:encoded:
After 90 minutes of play, the electronic board signaled 10 minutes of added time, and it was difficult to imagine at that point just how much drama would be packed into the coming minutes.

(Image credit: Mike Stewart)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Australian officials ask fans to respect the privacy of Neil, a trouble-making seal
item.description: The 5-year-old seal has a social media following twice the size of Tasmania's population, and his antics include bending traffic bollards and blocking roads.
item.content:encoded:
The 5-year-old seal has a social media following twice the size of Tasmania's population, and his antics include bending traffic bollards and blocking roads.

(Image credit: Sam Volker Photography)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
